# Spec context: §4.19 — Annotations

- Upstream: <https://abcnotation.com/wiki/abc:standard:v2.1#annotations>

- Subsection: 4.19 Annotations

## Preceding prose

> General text annotations can be added above, below or on the staff in a similar way to chord symbols. In this case, the string within double quotes is preceded by one of five symbols `^`, `_`, `` or `@` which controls where the annotation is to be placed; above, below, to the left or right respectiv
> Where two or more annotations with the same placement specifier are placed consecutively, e.g. for fingerings, the notation program should draw them on separate lines, with the first listed at the top.
> *Example:* The following annotations place the note between parentheses.
